name: Common Package Examples
description: Create runnable examples for the @algorandfoundation/algokit-utils/common subpackage. IMPORTANT: After implementing each example, you MUST run it to verify it works: `npx tsx <example>/index.ts`. The common package provides pure utility functions with NO LocalNet requirement - all examples work standalone without any network dependencies.

userStories.title: Infrastructure Setup - Verify Shared Utilities
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to verify shared utilities work and set up the common examples folder structure.
acceptanceCriteria: Verify examples/shared/utils.ts exists and exports: printHeader(), printStep(), printInfo(), printSuccess(), printError(), formatBytes(), formatHex()
Create examples/common folder if it doesn't exist
Verify the root examples/package.json includes @algorandfoundation/algokit-utils dependency
Verify the root examples/tsconfig.json has correct ESM module settings
npm install runs without errors in examples directory
TypeScript compiles without errors: npx tsc --noEmit
userStories.notes: No LocalNet required - common package is pure utilities

userStories.title: Address Basics Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to use the Address class for basic address operations including creation, validation, equality checks, and checksum computation.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/common/01-address-basics/index.ts
Demonstrate Address.fromString() to parse a base32 address string
Demonstrate Address.zeroAddress() to create the zero address
Show Address.toString() to encode back to base32 string
Demonstrate Address.equals() for comparing two addresses
Show Address.checksum() to compute the 4-byte checksum
Demonstrate isValidAddress() function for validation
Show the public key property (32-byte Uint8Array)
Display address constants: ALGORAND_ADDRESS_LENGTH (58), ALGORAND_ZERO_ADDRESS_STRING
Run the example with `npx tsx common/01-address-basics/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully
userStories.notes: No LocalNet required - pure utility functions

userStories.title: Address Encoding Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to encode and decode addresses between different formats and compute application addresses.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/common/02-address-encoding/index.ts
Demonstrate encodeAddress() to convert 32-byte public key to base32 string
Demonstrate decodeAddress() to convert base32 string to Address object
Demonstrate getApplicationAddress() to compute an app's escrow address from app ID
Show getAddress() helper function that accepts string, Address, or Addressable
Show round-trip: encode(decode(address)) equals original
Display the relationship between public key bytes and checksum
Run the example with `npx tsx common/02-address-encoding/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully
userStories.notes: No LocalNet required - pure utility functions

userStories.title: Array Utilities Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to use array utility functions for comparing and concatenating byte arrays.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/common/03-array-utilities/index.ts
Demonstrate arrayEqual() for comparing two arrays element-by-element
Show arrayEqual() with equal arrays returning true
Show arrayEqual() with different-length arrays returning false
Show arrayEqual() with same-length but different-content arrays returning false
Demonstrate concatArrays() for joining multiple Uint8Arrays
Show concatArrays() with multiple arrays of different sizes
Show that concatArrays() returns a new Uint8Array (doesn't modify inputs)
Run the example with `npx tsx common/03-array-utilities/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully
userStories.notes: No LocalNet required - pure utility functions

userStories.title: Constants Reference Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see all the protocol constants available in the common package for reference.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/common/04-constants/index.ts
Display transaction-related constants: TRANSACTION_DOMAIN_SEPARATOR, TRANSACTION_GROUP_DOMAIN_SEPARATOR, MAX_TRANSACTION_GROUP_SIZE
Display cryptographic constants: HASH_BYTES_LENGTH (32), PUBLIC_KEY_BYTE_LENGTH (32), SIGNATURE_BYTE_LENGTH (64)
Display address constants: ADDRESS_LENGTH (58), CHECKSUM_BYTE_LENGTH (4), TRANSACTION_ID_LENGTH (52)
Display application constants: MAX_EXTRA_PROGRAM_PAGES, PROGRAM_PAGE_SIZE, MAX_APP_ARGS, MAX_ARGS_SIZE
Display reference limits: MAX_OVERALL_REFERENCES, MAX_ACCOUNT_REFERENCES, MAX_APP_REFERENCES, MAX_ASSET_REFERENCES, MAX_BOX_REFERENCES
Display state schema limits: MAX_GLOBAL_STATE_KEYS, MAX_LOCAL_STATE_KEYS
Display asset limits: MAX_ASSET_NAME_LENGTH, MAX_ASSET_UNIT_NAME_LENGTH, MAX_ASSET_URL_LENGTH, MAX_ASSET_DECIMALS
Display encoding constants: LENGTH_ENCODE_BYTE_SIZE, BOOL_TRUE_BYTE, BOOL_FALSE_BYTE
Run the example with `npx tsx common/04-constants/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully
userStories.notes: No LocalNet required - pure constants display

userStories.title: Crypto Hash Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to use the SHA-512/256 hash function for computing Algorand-compatible hashes.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/common/05-crypto-hash/index.ts
Demonstrate hash() function for SHA-512/256 hashing
Hash a simple message and display the 32-byte result
Hash empty bytes and show the result
Show that hash() always returns exactly 32 bytes (HASH_BYTES_LENGTH)
Demonstrate hashing a transaction-like payload
Show hex representation of hash output
Verify determinism: same input always produces same hash
Run the example with `npx tsx common/05-crypto-hash/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully
userStories.notes: No LocalNet required - pure cryptographic function

userStories.title: Logger Type Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to implement and use the Logger interface for consistent logging across the SDK.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/common/06-logger/index.ts
Show the Logger type definition with all log levels: error, warn, info, verbose, debug
Create a simple console-based Logger implementation
Demonstrate calling each log level method
Show how to create a no-op logger (silent logger)
Show how to create a logger with custom formatting
Demonstrate the Logger interface compatibility with common logging patterns
Run the example with `npx tsx common/06-logger/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully
userStories.notes: No LocalNet required - pure type/interface example

userStories.title: JSON BigInt Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to parse and stringify JSON with BigInt support for handling large numbers safely.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/common/07-json-bigint/index.ts
Demonstrate parseJson() for parsing JSON strings with large numbers
Show that numbers > Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER are parsed as BigInt
Show that numbers <= Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER are parsed as regular numbers
Demonstrate stringifyJson() for serializing objects containing BigInt values
Show round-trip: stringifyJson(parseJson(str)) preserves large numbers
Compare with native JSON.parse() to show precision loss
Demonstrate with Algorand-relevant values like microAlgo amounts
Run the example with `npx tsx common/07-json-bigint/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully
userStories.notes: No LocalNet required - pure JSON utility functions

userStories.title: Msgpack Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to encode and decode MessagePack data, which is used for Algorand transaction encoding.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/common/08-msgpack/index.ts
Demonstrate encodeMsgpack() to serialize data to MessagePack format
Demonstrate decodeMsgpack() to deserialize MessagePack bytes
Show encoding simple objects with various types (strings, numbers, arrays)
Show that encodeMsgpack() sorts keys alphabetically (canonical encoding)
Show that decodeMsgpack() returns a Map by default for object data
Demonstrate encoding/decoding with BigInt values
Show encoding Uint8Array (bytes) values
Compare MessagePack size vs JSON size for same data
Run the example with `npx tsx common/08-msgpack/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully
userStories.notes: No LocalNet required - pure encoding utility functions

userStories.title: Primitive Codecs Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to use primitive codec types for encoding/decoding basic values in wire format.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/common/09-primitive-codecs/index.ts
Demonstrate numberCodec for encoding/decoding numbers
Demonstrate bigIntCodec for encoding/decoding BigInt values
Demonstrate booleanCodec for encoding/decoding boolean values
Demonstrate stringCodec for encoding/decoding UTF-8 strings
Demonstrate bytesCodec for encoding/decoding raw bytes
Demonstrate bytesBase64Codec for base64 encoding
Demonstrate addressCodec for encoding/decoding Algorand addresses
Show round-trip for each codec: decode(encode(value)) equals original
Show the Codec interface: encode(), decode() methods
Run the example with `npx tsx common/09-primitive-codecs/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully
userStories.notes: No LocalNet required - pure codec functions

userStories.title: Composite Codecs Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to use composite codec types for encoding/decoding arrays, maps, and records.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/common/10-composite-codecs/index.ts
Demonstrate ArrayCodec for encoding/decoding typed arrays
Show pre-built array codecs: numberArrayCodec, stringArrayCodec, bigIntArrayCodec, booleanArrayCodec, bytesArrayCodec, addressArrayCodec
Demonstrate MapCodec for encoding/decoding Map<K, V> types
Demonstrate RecordCodec for encoding/decoding Record<string, V> types
Show creating custom ArrayCodec with a specific element codec
Show encoding nested structures (array of arrays, map of arrays)
Verify round-trip encoding for each composite codec
Run the example with `npx tsx common/10-composite-codecs/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully
userStories.notes: No LocalNet required - pure codec functions

userStories.title: Model Codecs Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to use model codecs for encoding/decoding complex object structures with field metadata.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/common/11-model-codecs/index.ts
Demonstrate ObjectModelCodec for encoding/decoding typed objects
Show defining field metadata with FieldMetadata type
Demonstrate encoding format options: 'json' vs 'msgpack'
Demonstrate PrimitiveModelCodec for simple value types
Demonstrate ArrayModelCodec for array model types
Show how codecs handle optional fields
Show how codecs handle field renaming (wireKey vs property name)
Show round-trip encoding with ObjectModelCodec
Run the example with `npx tsx common/11-model-codecs/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully
userStories.notes: No LocalNet required - pure codec functions

userStories.title: Sourcemap Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to use ProgramSourceMap for mapping TEAL program counters to source locations for debugging.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/common/12-sourcemap/index.ts
Demonstrate ProgramSourceMap class construction from sourcemap data
Show the sourcemap format: version, sources, names, mappings
Demonstrate getPcs() to get all program counter values
Demonstrate getLocationForPc() to get source location for a specific PC
Show SourceLocation properties: line, column, sourceIndex, nameIndex
Demonstrate getPcsOnSourceLine() to find PCs for a source line
Show how sourcemaps enable TEAL debugging by mapping PC to source
Create a simple mock sourcemap to demonstrate the API
Run the example with `npx tsx common/12-sourcemap/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully
userStories.notes: No LocalNet required - pure sourcemap parsing

userStories.title: Verification Script
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a script to run all common package examples and verify they work.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/common/verify-all.sh bash script
Script runs each example in order (01 through 12)
Script reports success/failure for each example
Script exits with non-zero code if any example fails
All 12 examples pass when running ./verify-all.sh
Update root examples/verify-all.sh to include common examples if not already
userStories.notes: No LocalNet required - just runs TypeScript examples
